spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the headline Add presentationTime/paintTime to performance entries surface (paintTime, presentationTime, LCP); residual gaps in spec vocabulary: implementation-defined.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button, eventListener); controls include "Snapshot entries"; "Clear" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and some failure/feature-detect paths are surfaced, but the full set of edge cases (timeouts, abort, malformed input, multiple invocations) is not exercised.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5162859838046208 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on timing-comparison
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

summary: Solid concept page for Add presentationTime/paintTime to performance entries: interactive, design-system clean, and references the chromestatus entry. Tighten edge-case coverage and the spec-vocabulary surface area to take it from good to canonical.
